meta.setting: Mine gates, company stores, payroll wagons, strike roads, and French Gulch porches
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with hired force, company pressure, labor conflict, law heat, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, gold rush pressure, roads, labor, settlement, and resource conflict.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, courthouse context, and old town material.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Company Shotguns cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented payroll warning, guard-house note, or low-gun saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Mine Guard Men
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing private guards, payroll escorts, and strikebreakers whose authority comes from wages and weapons
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate company shotguns as intimidating but human hired force tied to mine owners, company stores, payroll, debt, and labor disputes.
Tie future moves to intimidation, escort duty, disarming, false arrest, strike pressure, fear, desertion, conscience under pay, and choosing when not to fire.
Avoid making them faceless villains; give them dirty cuffs, old injuries, debts, and reasons that do not excuse the work.
Let them create scenes where law, private money, and personal survival overlap.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Their page should make a badge feel optional and a paycheck feel heavier than a gun. A company guard is law with the county seal scratched off.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A company-shotgun scene should ask who paid for authority, who is afraid to cross the street, and which guard is tired enough to listen.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western labor-conflict and guard language only.
Keep private force human, paid, dangerous, and morally pressured.
The generated page must include image path images/company-shotguns.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make guards faceless monsters or clean heroes
do not use modern slang, modern police gear, automatic weapons, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about private force, payroll, labor pressure, intimidation, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
